Diagnosis of Cushing's syndrome with generalized linear model and development of mobile application

evidence_entities.text: Cushing syndrome (CS) is a potentially fatal disease caused by abnormally high levels of the cortisol hormone, first described by Harvey Cushing in 1912.
We developed a linear mathematical model with high predictive ability, achieving a classification accuracy of 97.03% and a Kappa value of 94.05%.
Features exhibiting a positive correlation of over 70% with CS include 2 mg (78.8%), 1 mg (76.9%), and mc (72.1%).
The most valuable biochemical test was found to be the 2 mg DST.

notes: This paper presents a machine learning approach for diagnosing Cushing's syndrome using biochemical tests and developing a mobile application for clinical decision support.
The study achieved 97.03% accuracy using a generalized linear model with various cortisol-related biomarkers.
The 2 mg dexamethasone suppression test was identified as the most valuable biochemical test for CS diagnosis.
